<!-- Change Proposal: Racketeering enterprise and enterprise roles -->
<!-- Target repository: CASE -->
<!-- Target release: 1.5.0 -->
<!-- Generated by CASE/UCO SDK draft_change_proposal tool -->

# Target release

**Target**: CASE/UCO 1.5.0

# Background

CASE/UCO has no way to represent the central objects of a racketeering (RICO) prosecution: the charged 'enterprise' and the functional roles its members serve. 18 U.S.C. § 1961(4) defines 'enterprise' to include any legal entity AND 'any union or group of individuals associated in fact although not a legal entity'; Boyle v. United States, 556 U.S. 938 (2009) requires only a purpose, relationships among associates, and longevity. Today producers bury the enterprise in a uco-identity:Organization description string, the division of labor (organizers, callers, money launderers, residential burglars, database hackers) in person descriptions, and the enumerated statutory predicate categories of the racketeering pattern (§ 1961(1)) in count prose — making cross-case queries such as 'all association-in-fact enterprises whose predicate categories include § 1343 wire fraud' or 'all persons serving a money-launderer role in any charged enterprise' impossible. Three additions close the gap: a RacketeeringEnterprise subclass of uco-identity:Organization with an enterpriseType open vocabulary ('legal-entity' / 'association-in-fact'), an EnterpriseRole subclass of uco-role:Role with a roleFunction open vocabulary, and a repeatable domain-free predicateStatute string property for the RICO count node. The concepts are jurisdiction-neutral (state racketeering analogues and non-US organized-crime statutes use the same enterprise/role/predicate structure) and are implemented and validated locally as extensions/rico/ in the CASE-UCO-Libraries repository.

**Related issues**: https://github.com/casework/CASE/issues/192, https://github.com/casework/CASE/issues/191

# Requirements

## Requirement 1

Define a new `RacketeeringEnterprise` UcoObject subclass as a subclass of `uco-identity:Organization`.


Properties:


- `enterpriseType` (xsd:string (0..1 recommended 1..1)): Statutory branch of the enterprise definition, open vocabulary: 'legal-entity' (an individual, partnership, corporation, association, or other legal entity) or 'association-in-fact' (a union or group of individuals associated in fact although not a legal entity). See 18 U.S.C. § 1961(4).

## Requirement 2

Define a new `EnterpriseRole` UcoObject subclass as a subclass of `uco-role:Role`.


Properties:


- `roleFunction` (xsd:string (1..1)): Function of the role within the enterprise, open vocabulary kept close to charging language: 'organizer', 'leader', 'database-hacker', 'target-identifier', 'caller', 'money-launderer', 'money-exchanger', 'residential-burglar', 'courier', 'enforcer', 'recruiter', 'straw-owner', 'promoter'.

## Requirement 3

Add property `predicateStatute` (xsd:string (0..*)) to `(domain-free; intended for criminal-charge nodes, e.g. the CriminalCharge concept stubbed in CASE issue #192)`. One statutory category of racketeering activity (18 U.S.C. § 1961(1)) enumerated in a RICO count as a predicate of the charged pattern, e.g. '18 U.S.C. § 1343 (wire fraud)'. Repeat once per enumerated category. A 'pattern of racketeering activity' requires at least two acts within ten years (§ 1961(5)).

# Risk / Benefit analysis

## Benefits

- Enables modeling of Racketeering enterprise and enterprise roles data in CASE/UCO-compliant graphs
- Improves interoperability between tools that encounter this data type
- The `RacketeeringEnterprise` class provides a structured representation where none currently exists
- The `EnterpriseRole` class provides a structured representation where none currently exists

## Risks

The submitter is unaware of risks associated with this change.

# Competencies demonstrated

## Competency 1

United States v. Lam et al., No. 1:24-cr-00417-CKK (D.D.C.): seventeen defendants charged with conspiring to conduct the affairs of the 'Social Engineering Enterprise' — a group of individuals associated in fact although not a legal entity, formed through online-gaming friendships — through a pattern of racketeering activity spanning eight enumerated statutory categories (§§ 1028, 1029, 1343, 1512, 1956, 1957, 1960, 2314). The indictment's 'Defendants' Roles in the Enterprise' section allocates members to functional roles: database hackers who obtained cryptocurrency-holder databases, organizers and target identifiers who collated them, callers who cold-called victims posing as exchange security staff (stealing approximately $245,093,239 from a single victim on 2024-08-19), money launderers who chain-hopped the proceeds into Monero on no-KYC exchanges and converted them to bulk cash and luxury goods, and a residential burglar who broke into a victim's New Mexico home hunting hardware wallets. An analyst needs to query: which persons served which functions in which enterprise; which charged enterprises are association-in-fact; and which RICO counts enumerate wire fraud among their predicates — none of which is possible when enterprise, roles, and predicates live in description strings.

### Competency Question 1.1

Which charged enterprises are association-in-fact enterprises?

#### Result 1.1

The SE Enterprise, with its `enterpriseType` value `"association-in-fact"`.

```sparql
PREFIX uco-core: <https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/core/>
PREFIX proposed: <http://example.org/ontology/proposed/>

SELECT ?enterprise ?name ?enterpriseType
WHERE {
    ?enterprise a proposed:RacketeeringEnterprise ;
                uco-core:name ?name ;
                proposed:enterpriseType ?enterpriseType .
    FILTER (?enterpriseType = "association-in-fact")
}
```

### Competency Question 1.2

Which persons serve which functions in which enterprise?

#### Result 1.2

Each person with a `Has_Role` relationship to an `EnterpriseRole`, the
role's `roleFunction` value, and the enterprise the role sits within via
its `Role_Within` relationship.

```sparql
PREFIX uco-core: <https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/core/>
PREFIX proposed: <http://example.org/ontology/proposed/>

SELECT ?person ?roleFunction ?enterprise
WHERE {
    ?hasRole a uco-core:Relationship ;
             uco-core:source ?person ;
             uco-core:target ?role ;
             uco-core:kindOfRelationship "Has_Role" .
    ?role a proposed:EnterpriseRole ;
          proposed:roleFunction ?roleFunction .
    ?roleWithin a uco-core:Relationship ;
                uco-core:source ?role ;
                uco-core:target ?enterprise ;
                uco-core:kindOfRelationship "Role_Within" .
}
```

### Competency Question 1.3

Which RICO counts enumerate wire fraud (18 U.S.C. § 1343) among their
predicate categories?

#### Result 1.3

Every charge node carrying a `predicateStatute` value containing "1343".

```sparql
PREFIX proposed: <http://example.org/ontology/proposed/>

SELECT ?charge ?predicate
WHERE {
    ?charge proposed:predicateStatute ?predicate .
    FILTER (CONTAINS(?predicate, "1343"))
}
```


# Example instance data

The example graph is also available as a standalone file at `change_proposals/racketeering-enterprise-and-enterprise-roles.jsonld` for validation and SPARQL testing.

```json
{
  "@context": {
    "kb": "http://example.org/kb/",
    "proposed": "http://example.org/ontology/proposed/",
    "uco-core": "https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/core/",
    "uco-identity": "https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/identity/",
    "uco-role": "https://ontology.unifiedcyberontology.org/uco/role/",
    "xsd": "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#"
  },
  "@graph": [
    {
      "@id": "kb:enterprise-1",
      "@type": ["proposed:RacketeeringEnterprise", "uco-identity:Organization"],
      "uco-core:name": "The Social Engineering Enterprise (SE Enterprise)",
      "uco-core:description": "Group of individuals associated in fact although not a legal entity (18 U.S.C. § 1961(4)), engaged in and affecting interstate and foreign commerce; operated from no later than October 2023 through at least May 2025. Purposes: stealing virtual currency through fraudulent pretenses; disguising the stolen funds through virtual-currency laundering; converting laundered funds to fiat and luxury goods.",
      "proposed:enterpriseType": "association-in-fact"
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:person-1",
      "@type": "uco-identity:Person",
      "uco-core:name": "Defendant One"
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:role-money-launderer",
      "@type": ["proposed:EnterpriseRole", "uco-role:Role"],
      "uco-core:name": "Money launderer for the SE Enterprise",
      "proposed:roleFunction": "money-launderer"
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:charge-count1",
      "@type": "uco-core:UcoObject",
      "uco-core:name": "Count 1: RICO Conspiracy (18 U.S.C. § 1962(d))",
      "uco-core:description": "Conspiring to conduct and participate in the conduct of the affairs of the SE Enterprise through a pattern of racketeering activity (18 U.S.C. §§ 1961(1), (5)). Typed as a plain UcoObject here because the CriminalCharge concept is itself a pending stub (CASE issue #192); predicateStatute is domain-free so it attaches to whichever charge class the committee adopts.",
      "proposed:predicateStatute": [
        "18 U.S.C. § 1028 (fraud and related activity in connection with identification documents)",
        "18 U.S.C. § 1029 (fraud and related activity in connection with access devices)",
        "18 U.S.C. § 1343 (wire fraud)",
        "18 U.S.C. § 1512 (tampering with a witness, victim, or an informant)",
        "18 U.S.C. § 1956 (laundering of monetary instruments)",
        "18 U.S.C. § 1957 (engaging in monetary transactions in property derived from specified unlawful activity)",
        "18 U.S.C. § 1960 (illegal money transmitters)",
        "18 U.S.C. § 2314 (interstate transportation of stolen property)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:rel-member-of-1",
      "@type": "uco-core:Relationship",
      "uco-core:source": [{"@id": "kb:person-1"}],
      "uco-core:target": [{"@id": "kb:enterprise-1"}],
      "uco-core:kindOfRelationship": "Member_Of",
      "uco-core:isDirectional": {"@type": "xsd:boolean", "@value": "true"}
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:rel-has-role-1",
      "@type": "uco-core:Relationship",
      "uco-core:source": [{"@id": "kb:person-1"}],
      "uco-core:target": [{"@id": "kb:role-money-launderer"}],
      "uco-core:kindOfRelationship": "Has_Role",
      "uco-core:isDirectional": {"@type": "xsd:boolean", "@value": "true"}
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:rel-role-within-1",
      "@type": "uco-core:Relationship",
      "uco-core:source": [{"@id": "kb:role-money-launderer"}],
      "uco-core:target": [{"@id": "kb:enterprise-1"}],
      "uco-core:kindOfRelationship": "Role_Within",
      "uco-core:isDirectional": {"@type": "xsd:boolean", "@value": "true"}
    },
    {
      "@id": "kb:rel-charged-with-1",
      "@type": "uco-core:Relationship",
      "uco-core:source": [{"@id": "kb:person-1"}],
      "uco-core:target": [{"@id": "kb:charge-count1"}],
      "uco-core:kindOfRelationship": "Charged_With",
      "uco-core:isDirectional": {"@type": "xsd:boolean", "@value": "true"}
    }
  ]
}
```

I am fine with my examples being transcribed and credited.
# Solution suggestion

* Define new class `RacketeeringEnterprise` as a subclass of `uco-identity:Organization`
* Define property `enterpriseType` with range `xsd:string (0..1 recommended 1..1)`
* Add SHACL shape for `RacketeeringEnterprise` with property constraints
* Define new class `EnterpriseRole` as a subclass of `uco-role:Role`
* Define property `roleFunction` with range `xsd:string (1..1)`
* Add SHACL shape for `EnterpriseRole` with property constraints
* Add property `predicateStatute` to `(domain-free; intended for criminal-charge nodes, e.g. the CriminalCharge concept stubbed in CASE issue #192)`
* Add unit test(s) demonstrating valid and invalid usage

# Pre-submission testing

Run `make test-proposal PROPOSAL=racketeering-enterprise-and-enterprise-roles` to execute all tests.

## SPARQL query testing

| Query | Tested | Expected results match | Notes |
|-------|--------|----------------------|-------|
| CQ 1.1 | Yes | Yes (1 result) | Returns the SE Enterprise with `enterpriseType` "association-in-fact" |
| CQ 1.2 | Yes | Yes (1 result) | Returns person → `money-launderer` → enterprise via Has_Role/Role_Within |
| CQ 1.3 | Yes | Yes (1 result) | Returns the RICO count with its § 1343 predicate value |

```
$ make test-proposal PROPOSAL=racketeering-enterprise-and-enterprise-roles

```

Validated with `--ontology-graph change_proposals/racketeering-enterprise-and-enterprise-roles.ttl`
(the proposal's class/property definitions). A full local implementation
with SHACL shapes and an exemplar A-Box lives in `extensions/rico/` and
validates against CASE 1.4.0 with `Conforms: True`; a 276-node worked
case graph exercising all three additions (17 defendants, 41 charges)
is at `examples/pacer/ddc_2024_cr_00417/lam-ddc-2024-racketeering.jsonld`.

## Unresolved issues

- Whether `RacketeeringEnterprise` belongs in CASE (investigation-centric,
  proposed here) or the concepts generalize into UCO's identity/role
  namespaces — committee guidance welcome.
- The final domain for `predicateStatute` depends on the CriminalCharge
  concept stubbed in CASE issue #192; it is proposed domain-free so it
  works with whichever charge class lands.
